ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT CLEASBY MANUFACTURING CO., INC.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO CONDUCT ORAL MENTAL STATUS EXAMINATION AND PSYCHOLOGINAL TESTING OF PLAINTIFF JOSE CAMPOS, JR.
MINI-MINUTES FOR SEP-24-2008 9:00 AM FOR DEPT 612

DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANT CLEASBY MANUFACTURING CO., INC.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO CONDUCT ORAL MENTAL STATUS EXAMINATION AND PSYCHOLOGICAL TESTING OF PLAINTIFF JOSE CAMPOS, JR. IS DENIED BASED UPON PLAINTIFF'S STIPULATION PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 2032.320(B). THE COURT DOES NOT FIND THAT THERE ARE EXCEPTIONAL CIRCUMSTANCES WHICH WOULD JUSTIFY A MENTAL EXAMINATION BASED UPON PLAINTIFF'S STIPULATION. NO CLAIM SHALL BE MADE FOR MENTAL AND EMOTIONAL DISTRESS OVER AND ABOVE THAT USUALLY ASSOCIATED WITH THE PHYSICAL INJURIES CLAIMED, AND NO EXPERT TESTIMONY SHALL BE OFFERED AT TRIAL IN SUPPORT OF THE CLAIM FOR DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATION OF FRANCIS D. CONWAY ARE OVERRULED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Ntc Of Mtn For Leave To Conduct Oral Mental Status Examination And Psychological Testing Of Pltf Jose Campos Jr.
Off Calendar Sep-24-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Francis D. Conway, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Cleasby Manufacturing Co., Inc.
Farzad Yassini, Esq., appeared by telephone for Plaintiff and moving party: Jose Campos

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant Cleasby Manufacturing Co., Inc.'s Motion For Leave To Conduct Oral Mental Status Examination And Psychological Testing Of Plaintiff Jose Campos, Jr. is denied based upon Plaintiff's stipulation pursuant to C.C.P. Section 2032.320(b). The court does not find that there are exceptional circumstances which would justify a mental examination based upon Plaintiff's stipulation. No claim shall be made for mental and emotional distress over and above that usually associated with the physical injuries claimed, and no expert testimony shall be offered at trial in support of the claim for damages. Plaintiff's objections to the declaration of Francis D. Conway are overruled. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
